original_prompt: abstract the hardware item type to be configurable, as an item types entity on it own
rephrased_goal: Create a new Item Types entity/view to configure hardware types dynamically, update the sidebar to navigate to it, and refactor items and product_types views to fetch these configured types from the database.

subtasks_required: Create a view with a table for listing item_types
Add a modal form for creating/editing item_types (fields: id, name)
tasks.analogy_reference: build a new storage room for molds
tasks.testing_criteria: view.item_types.js exports ItemTypesView that manages the 'item_types' collection.

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Register view in main.js and index.html
subtasks_required: Import ItemTypesView in main.js and map it to #/item_types
Add a link to #/item_types in index.html sidebar
tasks.analogy_reference: paint path to the new storage room
tasks.testing_criteria: Navigation to /#/item_types loads the new view.

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Refactor view.items.js to use item_types
subtasks_required: Populate the typeSelect dropdown with fetched types instead of hardcoded Pico/SIM
tasks.analogy_reference: use molds from the storage room
tasks.testing_criteria: Registering hardware shows dynamic types from the database.

tasks.description: Do-Pebble: Refactor view.product_types.js to use item_types
subtasks_required: Fetch item_types instead of extracting unique types from items
Render checkboxes based on the formal item_types collection
tasks.testing_criteria: Product Type creation dynamically pulls defined item types.

tasks.description: Sweep-Pebble: Ensure no hardcoded references remain
subtasks_required: Check for any other Pico/SIM hardcoded lists in form creation
